diary: Monday started like most days—quick shower, coffee, drive to Building 99. The morning standup was uneventful, but around 10am I realized the USA-Wales match was kicking off. Ducked out to Redmond Town Center with a couple coworkers and caught it at a café over lunch. The energy there was fun, even if the game itself wasn't thrilling. Grabbed a sandwich nearby and walked back to work, still buzzing a little. Afternoon was heads-down debugging—the kind of work that makes time disappear. Hit the gym after work to shake off the desk stiffness, then picked up dinner on the way home. Spent the evening winding down with shows and texting friends. Nothing extraordinary, but it was a solid day. Felt balanced somehow.
thought_process: I jumped at the chance to watch the match because, honestly, I needed a break from screens, even if I was just trading them for a café screen. The light rail closure didn't affect me much since I drive, but it made me grateful for that flexibility. Work felt productive but not draining—code reviews and debugging have a rhythm I've gotten comfortable with. The gym was important; I'd been sitting most of the day and needed to move. My mood stayed pretty even throughout—no highs or lows, just the steady hum of a regular Monday. I think the weather helped; sunny days always make the commute feel less tedious.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
